title: Legal transparency log at /transparency
description:
## Why

nie's threat model explicitly anticipates legal demands (Tor support, anonymous payment, no-KYC identity). A public transparency log serves two purposes: it deters speculative or frivolous demands by making them visible, and it gives users auditable evidence of government access attempts. This is standard practice for privacy-focused services (Canary Watch, Warrant Canary, Signal's transparency report).

## What

### Code changes (nie-relay)

- Add GET /transparency endpoint returning a static JSON file + rendered HTML
- The JSON schema: { entries: [{ id: 'LEG-0001', entity: string, type: 'subpoena|court_order|preservation', received: 'YYYY-MM-DD', status: 'responded|pending|challenged', notes: string }] }
- The file is hand-edited by the relay operator (no automated writes)
- Serve with appropriate Cache-Control headers

### Documentation

- LEGAL.md in the repo: explains what data the relay holds (pub_id hash, first_seen, subscription_expiry — nothing else), the operator's policy on billing under 18 USC 2706 (minimum statutory research fee), and commitment to publish all received demands

## Acceptance Criteria

- GET /transparency returns valid JSON and a human-readable HTML page
- An empty entries array is valid (initial state)
- LEGAL.md exists and accurately describes what data can be produced
- DESIGN.md §Legal Transparency documents what the relay can/cannot disclose
- The transparency endpoint is listed in README.md operator setup section
close_reason: Implemented: relay/src/transparency.rs with GET /transparency (JSON+HTML, Cache-Control: no-store, TRANSPARENCY_FILE env var); LEGAL.md created; README.md updated with env vars and transparency section
